title: Show join state for synced calendar events with active meetings
problem: When a synced Google Calendar event already has an active Minutia meeting, the calendar sidebar still shows Start meeting, which implies a duplicate meeting will be created.
user_story: As a meeting participant viewing a synced calendar event, I want the sidebar to recognize that the linked meeting is already live so I can join the active capture instead of starting a duplicate.
acceptance_criteria: If the calendar event is linked to a live Minutia meeting, the sidebar shows Meeting in progress.
The primary action label changes from Start meeting to Join now.
Join now opens the meeting URL when available and focuses the existing live Minutia capture tab or route.
The action must not create a duplicate meeting for the same synced calendar event.
Regression coverage verifies the sidebar live state for a synced event with meetingStatus live.
product_note: This is a trust signal in the calendar workflow. The sidebar should reflect the canonical meeting state, not only the calendar event state.

implementation: Calendar sidebar details now show Meeting in progress for synced events linked to live Minutia meetings.
The primary action changes to Join now and routes to the existing live capture without calling the calendar start endpoint.
Meeting cache invalidation now refreshes month-level sidebar data after start, join, or end mutations.
verification: pnpm exec playwright test e2e/regression/google-calendar-agenda.spec.ts --project=chromium --grep "shows join state"
GitHub CI: Dependency Audit, Lint & Typecheck, E2E Tests 1/16 through 16/16

title: Reduce process fatigue with frictionless capture
problem: Minutia requires manual discipline to log updates. During chaotic sprints, teams skip logging and the system becomes unreliable, causing reversion to old tools like spreadsheets.
user_story: As a team member, I want to capture meeting updates so quickly and effortlessly that it beats opening a scratchpad or Slack message, ensuring the system stays reliable even during chaos.
acceptance_criteria: Slack/Teams bot integration for async logging without leaving chat
Quick-add keyboard shortcuts accessible from any screen
Calendar auto-draft agenda items from event descriptions
Auto-create follow-up tasks from meeting decisions
product_note: This addresses the core reliability issue: if entry isn't near-frictionless, the system fails during stress. Quick wins first (Slack bot, shortcuts), then deeper integrations (voice-to-text, AI-assisted generation).

title: Bridge decisions to execution tools
problem: Minutia tracks agenda items (what's owed) but actual work happens in GitHub Issues or Jira. Without bridging this gap, Minutia becomes a passive duplicate layer rather than an active bridge.
user_story: As a developer, I want high-fidelity decisions from Minutia to automatically flow into my execution tools (GitHub/Jira) so I don't have to manually duplicate work.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub Issues integration: sync agenda items to issues, push decisions back
Jira integration: bidirectional sync between Minutia decisions and Jira tickets
Webhooks/API to push decisions to any execution tool
Auto-create follow-up tasks from meeting decisions
VS Code extension for in-IDE capture
product_note: Start with GitHub sync (high impact, medium effort), then expand to Jira and generic webhooks. Long-term: IDE integration to make Minutia the source of truth.
